Â Â Lukas Sieper (NI). â Signora Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, cari cittadini d'Europa, l'energia Ã¨ la linfa della nostra societÃ  ma oggi le nostre reti elettriche sono troppo vecchie, troppo lente e troppe isolate.

Se vogliamo piÃ¹ energia rinnovabile, auto elettriche e industrie pulite, abbiamo bisogno di una rete elettrica europea forte, moderna e connessa. Una rete che unisca il sole del Sud, il vento del Nord e la forza dell'innovazione ovunque.

Per farlo servono investimenti, collaborazione tra gli Stati e meno burocrazia. Non possiamo permetterci dei ritardi. Il clima cambia, i costi aumentano e la sicurezza energetica Ã¨ una sfida globale. Una rete europea dell'elettricitÃ  non Ã¨ solo un progetto tecnico, Ã¨ una promessa di solidarietÃ , sostenibilitÃ  e sovranitÃ .

Â 
Â Â 
Â Â 

(Fine della procedura "catch the eye")

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Ekaterina Zaharieva, Member of the Commission. â Madam President, honourable Members, dear rapporteur, I would like once again to express my gratitude for this debate. The fact that so many of you participated shows how important this topic is for our competitiveness, our security, our autonomy and the everyday life of our citizens, so I very much appreciated all of your contributions and opinions.

What I will take from this debate is that â no matter that there will be different opinions on what type of energy we should develop â the grids' expansion and modernisation is really the backbone, the crossroad, no matter what we call it, of the many measures that we need to achieve affordable, secure and decarbonised energy. And we should really increase our investment and speed up the processes of permission.

I also will say that all of us know the transformation of our energy system is a complex and multi-phase challenge and requires coordinated efforts from all stakeholders involved. So many of you mentioned that we have discussed these energy unions for years, and we are we are still not there. But I really believe that the momentum is very, very important and I see a big support, as I said already, by voting yesterday for your excellent report, dear Anna, so I really believe that we can achieve our goals together.

So, I'm pleased to see that we have a reliable partner in this House, and I look forward to continuing our cooperation to address the challenges and opportunities that lie ahead of us.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Anna StÃ¼rgkh, rapporteur. â MadamÂ President, I would like to say thank you, Commissioner, and thank you, colleagues, for this debate.

Now, I heard that the far-right in this House is quite worried, so let me address some of those worries. You seem to be quite shocked by the numbers of the investment needed: EURÂ 584 billion by 2030. Yes, that is a lot of money. But let me put it in perspective: EURÂ 1.8 trillion by 2030 is what we will spend on fossil fuel imports if we don't change our system. And you as patriots, as nationalists, you must be pleased to hear that we can invest in a grid with only EURÂ 584 billion, keeping that money in Europe instead of sending EURÂ 1.8 trillion at the same time to other countries, keeping it in Europe, benefiting European companies, benefiting European workers.

I also heard the worry that we need to make sure that interconnections really serve the Member States. Now, I am pleased that this shows support for our report because, let me quote, 'we are asking for binding interconnections based on a needs assessment, to make sure that we build interconnectors that are needed and not just thought of'.

I also heard a lot of worry about the energy mix, about nuclear being demonised. Well, I've read this report a couple of times and let me assure you: nowhere in this report is there a demonisation of nuclear. Rather, there is even a mention of the prerogative of nation states to choose their own energy mix. And let me be very clear, when Ms Knafo was saying, 'you don't want nuclear' â well, yes, in my country, for example, we don't want nuclear. But again, isn't that great? It's our prerogative to choose our own energy mix.

I also heard worries about fishers suffering from offshore wind; however, we see that they are actually thriving in offshore farms.

So, let me thank you again for this debate. I hope I lifted some of those worries, and I'm really looking forward not only to the vote tomorrow, but to working with all of you on the implementation and with you, Commissioner, and the rest of the Commission on getting this forward.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Presidente. â La discussione Ã¨ chiusa.

La votazione si svolgerÃ  giovedÃ¬ 19 giugno 2025.

Dichiarazioni scritte (articolo 178)

Â 
Â Â

Azonban ahogy a Parlament Ãºj, elektromos hÃ¡lÃ³zatokrÃ³l szÃ³lÃ³ jelentÃ©se hangsÃºlyozza, jelenleg a vezetÃ©kÃ¡llomÃ¡ny elavult; az engedÃ©lyeztetÃ©si Ã©s Ã©pÃ­tÃ©si folyamat pedig tÃºlsÃ¡gosan lassÃº.

Jelenleg kapacitÃ¡shiÃ¡ny miatt 2029-ig nem lehet Ãºj szÃ©lerÅmÅ±veket, illetve 2030-ig a hÃ¡lÃ³zatra betÃ¡plÃ¡lÃ³ Ãºj napelemparkokat a hÃ¡lÃ³zatra kapcsolni. Pedig az OrbÃ¡n-rezsim korrupciÃ³s kockÃ¡zatai miatt visszatartott EU-s helyreÃ¡llÃ­tÃ¡si alap forrÃ¡sait Ã©pp ehhez hasonlÃ³ elektrifikÃ¡ciÃ³s fejlesztÃ©sekre lehetett volna felhasznÃ¡lni.

Egyes szakÃ©rtÅk szerint olyan napokon amikor az erÅs napsÃ¼tÃ©s miatt MagyarorszÃ¡g is szinte kizÃ¡rÃ³lag napelemekkel termel Ã¡ramot, nem elkÃ©pzelhetetlen egy spanyolorszÃ¡gihoz hasonlÃ³ orszÃ¡gos Ã¡ramszÃ¼net.

A hÃ¡lÃ³zat problÃ©mÃ¡inak orvosolÃ¡sa Ã©s a megfizethetÅ Ã¡ramÃ¡rak biztosÃ­tÃ¡sa mindannyiunkat Ã©rintÅ kÃ©rdÃ©s. Â Â Waldemar Tomaszewski (ECR), raÅ¡tu. â Ponia Pirmininke, daugiau kaip 40Â % ES skirstomÅ³jÅ³ tinklÅ³ yra senesni nei 40 metÅ³. Tai reiÅ¡kia iÅ¡Å¡Å«kius ir iÅ¡laidas ateityje, susijusias su energetikos tinklÅ³ ir mazgÅ³ rekonstrukcija. Remiantis Europos Komisijos duomenimis, iki 2030Â m. Ä¯ elektros tinklus turÄtÅ³ bÅ«ti investuota 584Â mlrd. EUR. AtsiÅ¾velgiant Ä¯ auganÄiÄ elektros energijos paklausÄ Europos SÄjungoje, bÅ«tinos didelÄs investicijos ir energetikos tinklÅ³ modernizavimas, siekiant padidinti tarpvalstybinius ir nacionalinius perdavimo pajÄgumus. TaÄiau reikia nepamirÅ¡ti ir pasirÅ«pinti, kad infrastruktÅ«ros modernizavimas nelemtÅ³ elektros energijos kainÅ³, kurios Europoje ir taip yra labai aukÅ¡tos, padidÄjimo. Paprasti Å¾monÄs negali nukentÄti vykstant pokyÄiams, kurie mÅ«sÅ³ laukia Å¡iame ekonomikos sektoriuje, jie negali mokÄti uÅ¾ blogus ES sprendimus. Lygiai taip pat pigios energijos reikia maÅ¾oms ir vidutinÄms Ä¯monÄms, kurios yra ES ekonomikos ir verslumo pagrindas. Europa Å¡iandien negali sau leisti eksperimentuoti energetikos srityje. Jei SÄjunga nesusidoros su Å¡iomis realijomis, jos ekonomika patirs vis didesnes iÅ¡laidas.

13. Composition of committees and delegations
Video of the speeches
Â 

Â Â Presidente. â Il gruppo ECR ha comunicato alla Presidente una decisione di modifica relativamente alle nomine in una commissione. Questa decisione sarÃ  inclusa nel verbale della seduta odierna ed entrerÃ  in vigore a decorrere dal presente annuncio.

Â 

14. Rise in violence and the deepening humanitarian crisis in South Sudan (debate)
Video of the speeches
Â 

Â Â Presidente. â L'ordine del giorno reca la discussione sulla dichiarazione della Commissione sull'aumento della violenza e aggravarsi della crisi umanitaria in Sud Sudan (2025/2751(RSP)).

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Ekaterina Zaharieva, Member of the Commission. â Madam President, honourable Members of the European Parliament, thank you for the opportunity to address this Chamber on the situation in South Sudan, which we follow with great concern. The euphoria at the time of independence in 2011 has long gone, unfortunately. Instead of peace and economic advancement, South Sudan remains trapped in a cycle of violence, displacement, human suffering and prolonged instability.

Since February, we have been witnessing an upsurge in violence, including bombing on health facilities and attacks against civilians, which is fuelling a deepening humanitarian crisis. More than 9Â million people â over 70Â % of the population â depend on humanitarian assistance. Over 2Â million are internally displaced. Also, since the outbreak of the civil war in neighbouring Sudan, 1.2Â million have fled to South Sudan, straining already overstretched resources. This represents a burden on neighbouring countries and has potential consequences on migration flows.

The European Union is deeply concerned by the deteriorating security and humanitarian situation. We strongly condemn the recent violence in Upper Nile, Unity, Warrap and Jonglei. These are not isolated incidents, unfortunately â they reflect a broader erosion of the fragile peace established under the 2018 revitalised peace agreement, a peace that has been repeatedly violated, especially this year. The arrest of the first viceâpresident and members of his party has dangerously escalated the political crisis, pushing the country to the brink of civil war. Progress in implementing the peace agreement remains limited. Civic and political space is shrinking and impunity persists.

South Sudan's leaders bear full responsibility for the well-being of their people. The international community cannot build peace for them, but we can and we will support those working for stability, justice and human dignity. Indeed, the EU is not a distant actor in this crisis. Besides the efforts of our delegation in Juba, our Special Representative for the Horn of Africa, Annette Weber, has been at the forefront of diplomatic engagement. In early April, she toured the region and met with all key stakeholders in Juba to urge them to resolve their disputes through dialogues. However, she was denied access to Riek Machar, who remains under house arrest. This underscores the challenging political climate we face.

Nevertheless, we remain committed to using the diplomatic means at our disposal to help South Sudan move forward. We continue to provide sustainable humanitarian aid, and we remain one of the largest donors of development funding to South Sudan. Yet access to humanitarian organisations is increasingly obstructed by insecurity and bureaucratic obstacles. We call on the leaders of South Sudan to show political will to implement the peace agreement without delay, to release all political detainees, to refrain from incitement and ensure free humanitarian access.Â We support the work of the Intergovernmental Authority on Development, the African Union and the United Nations Mission in South Sudan in advancing these goals.

The people of South Sudan have suffered enough. They deserve a future free from violence, hunger and fear, a future built on peace, accountability and democratic governance. The EU stands ready to accompany South Sudan on this path, but the responsibility to act lies first and foremost with its leaders.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Michael Gahler, on behalf of the PPE Group. â Madam President, colleagues, while the world's attention primarily focuses on the Israeli-Iran war, on the situation in Gaza and Russia's continued aggression against Ukraine, South Sudan, the youngest nation in the world, is at the brink of yet another civil war. Political and ethnic tensions have flared up in recent months, leading to direct military confrontations between the South Sudanese army, under President Salva Kiir, and the White Army, linked to First ViceâPresident Riek Machar and his opposition force.

The Commission has said 9.3 million South Sudanese are in need of humanitarian assistance, while around 2 million are already internally displaced. And yet, while war is already raging in the countries neighbouring South Sudan, a full-scale war could still be prevented in South Sudan. Measures taken or not taken now by the South Sudanese leaders, by regional international actors and the EU will decide whether South Sudan goes down the path of its neighbours or embarks on a road towards peace and stability.

In order to stabilise the country, first, South Sudanese leaders must return to the 2018 peace agreement, which is at serious risk of collapse. Arbitrarily detained opposition figures â above all, ViceâPresident Machar â must be released, military operations by the South Sudanese army and opposition forces must be stopped, as a prerequisite for both warring parties to engage in dialogue.

Second, foreign actors must stop fuelling the conflict and fully retreat from South Sudanese territory. At the request of President Kiir, Ugandan forces have fought alongside the South Sudanese army, providing heavy military equipment for aerial bombardments, as well as 2Â 000 soldiers for ground offensives. While thanks to the pressure of the international community, particularly the African countries and the EU, Ugandan forces have become less visible in the country, they must fully retreat.

At the same time, the recent rapprochement of President Kiir with the Sudanese paramilitary and its regional sponsor, United Arab Emirates, presents a risk of regionalisation of the Sudanese civil war. Let us not let it happen, but do the utmost diplomatically for a peaceful way forward.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Marit Maij, namens de S&D-Fractie. â Voorzitter, commissaris, Zuid-Sudan is een vruchtbaar land, rijk aan bodemschatten. En toch is het een van de armste landen ter wereld. Al vele decennia, en zeker sinds de onafhankelijkheid, wordt Zuid-Sudan geteisterd door oorlog en conflicten. Burgerdorpen worden gebombardeerd. Meisjes en vrouwen worden verkracht. Seksueel geweld wordt ingezet als wapen en politieke tegenstanders worden opgesloten zonder proces.

Meer dan 2Â miljoen mensen zijn ontheemd en ruim 9Â miljoen Zuid-Sudanezen zijn afhankelijk van humanitaire hulp. Toch worden zelfs medische posten aangevallen, waaronder recent een kliniek van Artsen zonder Grenzen.

Buitenlandse machten en corrupte leiders hebben baat bij deze instabiele situatie, omdat deze hen gemakkelijk toegang geeft tot de bodemschatten van Zuid-Sudan.

De Europese Unie moet inderdaad humanitaire hulp en toegang daartoe veilig stellen. Zij moet vrouwenorganisaties ter plaatse steunen, de diplomatieke druk op de leiders opvoeren en zich inzetten om het vredesakkoord van 2018 nieuw leven in te blazen.

Zuid-Sudanezen verdienen geen stilte. Zij verdienen solidariteit en gerechtigheid.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â GyÃ¶rgy HÃ¶lvÃ©nyi, a PfE kÃ©pviselÅcsoport nevÃ©ben. â Tisztelt ElnÃ¶k Asszony! 9,5 milliÃ³ ember. Ennyien szorulnak az itt emlÃ­tÃ©sre kerÃ¼lt humanitÃ¡rius segÃ­tsÃ©gre DÃ©l-SzudÃ¡nban. KÃ©tmilliÃ³ belsÅ menekÃ¼lt, hÃ©tmilliÃ³ Ã©hezÅ ember. A hiteles eurÃ³pai fellÃ©pÃ©s azon mÃºlik, hogy valÃ³s kÃ©pessÃ©geinkhez mÃ©rten hatÃ¡rozzuk meg szerepvÃ¡llalÃ¡sunkat. Most, amikor a mezÅgazdasÃ¡gi szezon kÃ¶zepÃ©n kiÃºjult fegyveres konfliktus miatt a gazdÃ¡k nem tudjÃ¡k megmÅ±velni a fÃ¶ldjeiket, a legsÃ¼rgetÅbb kihÃ­vÃ¡s az Ã©hÃ­nsÃ©g enyhÃ­tÃ©se.

Egymagunk azonban nem rendelkezÃ¼nk ezekhez szÃ¼ksÃ©ges eszkÃ¶zÃ¶kkel. Ãppen ezÃ©rt minÃ©l hamarabb szÃ¼ksÃ©gÃ¼nk van arra, hogy mÃ¡s humanitÃ¡rius partnerek, kÃ¼lÃ¶nÃ¶sen pedig, akÃ¡rmilyen nehÃ©z is, a transzatlanti partnereinkkel egyÃ¼ttmÅ±kÃ¶dve lÃ©pjÃ¼nk fel az Ã©hÃ­nsÃ©g enyhÃ­tÃ©sÃ©re. Meg kell talÃ¡lni az egyÃ¼ttmÅ±kÃ¶dÃ©s lehetÅsÃ©geit. Ezt vÃ¡rjuk a BizottsÃ¡gtÃ³l.

KÃ©pviselÅtÃ¡rsaim! A dÃ©l-szudÃ¡ni humanitÃ¡rius igÃ©nyek kielÃ©gÃ­tÃ©sÃ©re tovÃ¡bbi 1,4 milliÃ¡rd dollÃ¡rra lenne szÃ¼ksÃ©g. Csakhogy az UniÃ³ ezt a forrÃ¡st nem tudja elÅteremteni. KÃ¶zvetÃ­tÅkÃ©nt viszont hitelesek lehetÃ¼nk a pÃ¡rbeszÃ©d elÅmozdÃ­tÃ¡sÃ¡ban, a megbÃ©kÃ©lÃ©s elÅsegÃ­tÃ©sÃ©ben. Ãgy vethetÃ¼nk gÃ¡tat a humanitÃ¡rius vÃ¡lsÃ¡g mÃ©lyÃ¼lÃ©sÃ©nek. Ebben kell a kÃ¼lÃ¼gyi szolgÃ¡latnak eredmÃ©nyt elÃ©rnie. Ez az, ami valÃ³ban hitelessÃ© teheti az UniÃ³ fellÃ©pÃ©sÃ©t nemcsak ott, hanem sajÃ¡t polgÃ¡raink elÅtt.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Adam Bielan, on behalf of the ECR Group. â Madam President, dear Commissioner, South Sudan is once again on the edge of collapse. Since February, fighting between government forces and opposition groups has erupted again and devastated the Upper Nile, Jonglei and the Unity State. Entire communities have been displaced, hospitals destroyed and civilians killed. The humanitarian situation is dire. Over 165Â 000 people have fled in recent months, adding to the more than 44Â million South Sudanese who are now displaced. Overall, nearly 70Â % of South Sudan's population â some 9.3 million people â require urgent assistance, yet humanitarian access is increasingly constrained by insecurity, targeted attacks on aid workers and the looting of vital supplies.

In parallel to the struggle to deliver food and medical aid, there are attacks on human dignity and religious freedom. Christians are facing targeted violence, intimidation and the destruction of places of worship.

Colleagues, the international community cannot stand by while this crisis unfolds. The EU and international institutions must de-escalate violence, protect civilians and ensure unimpeded humanitarian access. We call on the Commission and the EEAS to link every euro of additional funding to verifiable, safe, rapid and unimpeded access for humanitarian agencies.

Let us not forget that behind these numbers are real people. The time for decisive action is now.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Jan-Christoph Oetjen, im Namen der Renew-Fraktion. â Frau PrÃ¤sidentin, verehrte Kolleginnen und Kollegen! Der Sudan ist ein krisengeschÃ¼tteltes Land, eines der Ã¤rmsten LÃ¤nder dieser Welt, und seit der UnabhÃ¤ngigkeit des SÃ¼dsudans gab es nach einer kurzen Phase der Ruhe eigentlich auch dort nur eine sehr, sehr schlimme humanitÃ¤re Situation. Wir mÃ¼ssen uns vor Augen halten, dass Menschen vertrieben werden, dass Menschen hungern und dass es keinen Zugang fÃ¼r Hilfsorganisationen gibt in die betroffenen Regionen, die dafÃ¼r sorgen kÃ¶nnen, dass Menschen geholfen wird. Ich mÃ¶chte mich bei der Kommission ausdrÃ¼cklich dafÃ¼r bedanken, dass so klare Worte gefunden wurden Ã¼ber die Situation im SÃ¼dsudan.

Ich glaube, dass wir als EuropÃ¤ische Union alles dafÃ¼r tun mÃ¼ssen, dass das, was im Friedensabkommen von 2018 dargelegt wurde, auch tatsÃ¤chlich weiter unterstÃ¼tzt wird und wieder neue Traktion gewinnt, damit dieses Land zur Ruhe kommen kann. Gleichzeitig wollen wir als Parlament die sÃ¼dsudanesische Regierung dazu auffordern, endlich alle Attacken gegen die ZivilbevÃ¶lkerung zu unterlassen, dafÃ¼r zu sorgen, dass humanitÃ¤re Hilfsorganisationen in die entsprechenden Gebiete kommen, dass die, die politisch gefangen genommen wurden, einen ordentlichen Prozess bekommen und dass sie, wenn sie einfach so festgenommen wurden, auch wieder freigelassen werden.

Der Rechtsstaat muss auch im SÃ¼dsudan gelten, auch in den schÃ¤rfsten Krisenzeiten. Wir wollen als EuropÃ¤ische Union alles dazu beitragen, dass sich die Situation verbessertÂ â mit der Hilfe, die wir leisten kÃ¶nnenÂ â, aber den wichtigsten ersten Schritt, den muss die Regierung vor Ort selber tun.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Erik Marquardt, im Namen der Verts/ALE-Fraktion. â Frau PrÃ¤sidentin, Frau Kommissarin! Ich kann nur allen Vorrednerinnen und Vorrednern zustimmen. Es ist eine unglaubliche Situation, die so viel Leid erzeugt hat im SÃ¼dsudan, dass wir inzwischen ja Ã¼ber die grÃ¶Ãte humanitÃ¤re Krise unserer Zeit reden mÃ¼ssen. Ich finde es richtig, dass wir heute darÃ¼ber reden. Ich finde es richtig, die Zahlen anzusprechen, auch dass Menschen dahinter stecken, aber ich wollte meine Rede eigentlich auf einen anderen Aspekt lenken. Ich glaube nÃ¤mlich, dass es am Ende schon auch so ist, dass wir nicht nur hier in den Plenarreden Dinge fordern dÃ¼rfen, sondern wir mÃ¼ssen unsere Hausaufgaben als Parlament dann auch machen.

Wenn ich mir zum Beispiel anschaue, dass wir als Parlament aktuell nicht mal in der Lage sind, eine klare Position zu finden angesichts des Wegbrechens der USâamerikanischen Entwicklungszusammenarbeit und humanitÃ¤rer Hilfe, dass wir nicht in der Lage sind, uns hier auf eine Position zu einigen, die eine starke, auch europÃ¤ische Stimme, eine Stimme des EuropÃ¤ischen Parlaments dann zum Beispiel bei der anstehenden Konferenz in Sevilla ausdrÃ¼ckt, sich fÃ¼r Entwicklungszusammenarbeit, aber auch fÃ¼r humanitÃ¤re Hilfe einsetzt, dann frage ich mich schon, ob wir eigentlich alles dafÃ¼r getan habenÂ â auch wir als Parlamentarierinnen und ParlamentarierÂ â, dass solche Krisen richtig angegangen werden. Ich wÃ¼nsche mir, dass wir in Zukunft da besser sind, als wir es gestern waren.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Ãzlem Demirel, im Namen der Fraktion The Left. â Frau PrÃ¤sidentin! Die UN hat die Situation im Sudan und ihre Folgen fÃ¼r die NachbarlÃ¤nder, also auch SÃ¼dsudan, als die grÃ¶Ãte humanitÃ¤re Krise der Welt bezeichnet, aber das scheint kaum jemanden wirklich zu scheren. Dabei sieht man, wenn man heute auf SÃ¼dsudan guckt, ein trauriges Beispiel europÃ¤ischer Politik. Der Zustand in dieser Region ist auch Folge eines klassischen Stellvertreterkrieges, und europÃ¤ische Staaten waren nicht unbeteiligt daran.

Die EU spielte eine aktive Rolle bei der Abspaltung des SÃ¼dsudans, trotz mehrfacher Warnungen, dass es zu MachtkÃ¤mpfen fÃ¼hren kÃ¶nnte. Die EU bildete auch SicherheitskrÃ¤fte im SÃ¼dsudan aus, die heute Gewaltakte gegenÃ¼ber der BevÃ¶lkerung ausÃ¼ben. Es heiÃt, es handele sich bei dem Konflikt um einen ethnischen religiÃ¶sen Konflikt, doch es ging die ganze Zeit im Konflikt um SÃ¼dsudan und Sudan um Gold- und ErdÃ¶lvorkommen.

Auch europÃ¤ische Staaten waren schnell dabei, die Abspaltung voranzutreiben, um dazu Zugang zu haben. Und jetzt schauen die europÃ¤ischen Institutionen weg, wenn wir die grÃ¶Ãte humanitÃ¤re Katastrophe dort haben. Das ist nicht akzeptabel, daran muss sich schnell etwas Ã¤ndern.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Ingeborg Ter Laak (PPE). â Voorzitter, commissaris, gekidnapt, misbruikt en voor dood achtergelaten worden... Dit is het verhaal van heel veel kindsoldaten in Sudan. In de hoop de dood van een familielid te wreken of toch wat eten te krijgen, komen ze terecht bij gewapende groeperingen. Eenmaal binnen worden ze als seksobject of menselijk schild gebruikt of worden ze bewapend als soldaat. Al deze kinderen zijn beschadigd voor de rest van hun leven.

De mensenrechtensituatie in Sudan is ronduit verschrikkelijk. Burgers en ziekenhuizen worden aangevallen en er wordt geplunderd. Meer dan 2Â miljoen mensen zijn op de vlucht geslagen. Mensen worden verkracht en vermoord.

Tegelijkertijd blijven de voedselprijzen explosief stijgen, wat mensen tot wanhoop drijft. Het gevolg is dat bijna 8Â miljoen mensen kampen met ernstige voedseltekorten. 2,3Â miljoen mensen verkeren zelfs in een acute voedselcrisis en zijn echt ondervoed.

Europa moet hiertegen in actie komen. De oorzaak van dit alles is de strijd om grondstoffen en olie en de inmenging van buitenlandse actoren.

Er moet worden gebouwd aan vrede, samen met vrouwen en mannen uit de verschillende Sudanese gemeenschappen.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Francisco Assis (S&D). â Senhora Presidente, Senhora ComissÃ¡ria, caras e caros colegas, enquanto nos mobilizamos e nos indignamos, aliÃ¡s, com inteira justiÃ§a, face ao sofrimento de outros povos, continuamos a menosprezar o sofrimento do povo sudanÃªs.

Bombardeamentos indiscriminados, utilizaÃ§Ã£o sistemÃ¡tica de violÃªncia sexual e restriÃ§Ãµes Ã  ajuda humanitÃ¡ria. HÃ¡, pelo menos, 100 000 mortos e 11 milhÃµes de deslocados.

NÃ£o hÃ¡, neste momento, qualquer sinal por parte da UniÃ£o Europeia de esforÃ§os diplomÃ¡ticos consistentes, tendo em vista a resoluÃ§Ã£o deste problema. NÃ£o hÃ¡ uma responsabilizaÃ§Ã£o dos atores regionais que estÃ£o a alimentar o conflito ââ a comeÃ§ar pelos Emirados Ãrabes Unidos, o principal apoio das milÃ­cias paramilitares que tentam assumir o controlo do paÃ­s.

A UniÃ£o Europeia nÃ£o foi ainda sequer capaz de impor sanÃ§Ãµes contra os principais responsÃ¡veis pelo ciclo de violÃªncia no SudÃ£o e contra os principais responsÃ¡veis tambÃ©m pelo fornecimento e aquisiÃ§Ã£o de armas, apesar das vÃ¡rias recomendaÃ§Ãµes emanadas deste Parlamento nesse sentido.

HaverÃ¡, talvez, um lugar nos futuros livros de HistÃ³ria para salientar a indiferenÃ§a da comunidade internacional face a esta guerra horrenda, a mais mortÃ­fera do nosso tempo.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Barry Andrews (Renew). â Madam President, Commissioner, colleagues, I've been to South Sudan a couple of times and it's really striking: the inaccessibility and remoteness of the country, areas with no paved roads about the size of Belgium. It's really quite an extraordinary place.

And it's great that we're having this debate, but we have to be realistic, and we're having this debate in the context of US withdrawal â this has impacted NGOs that are delivering humanitarian aid on the ground â and the withdrawal of EU Member States cumulatively and individually. That has had a massive impact.

And there's very little that the Commission can do, the European Commission has really stayed the course here. But there are two things that I would call on the Commission to do.

First of all, South Sudan is very much a fragile state affected by conflict, disease outbreak, etc., and it really underlines how urgently we need a fragility framework.

Secondly, I would call on the Commission to push back hard on the proposal to downsize the EU delegation in South Sudan. As violence and famine spread across the country, I am sure you will agree, Commissioner, that it sends out a very bad signal that the EU will withdraw after the US withdrawal.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Murielle Laurent (S&D). â Madame la PrÃ©sidente, Madame la Commissaire, chers collÃ¨gues, le Soudan du Sud est Ã  nouveau dans une situation critique. Imaginez un instant plus de 165Â 000Â personnes dÃ©placÃ©es en seulement trois mois. L'accord de paix deÂ 2018 vacille Ã  nouveau. Les violences s'intensifient et les tensions politiques s'aggravent Ã  l'approche des Ã©lections. Ce sont toujours les mÃªmes qui paient le prix, les civils. Les populations souffrent et nous connaissons les consÃ©quences: famines, Ã©pidÃ©mies, dÃ©placements, violences sexuelles. Et oÃ¹ iront les populations dans des territoires dÃ©jÃ  en grande difficultÃ©?

L'Union europÃ©enne se doit d'Ãªtre Ã  la hauteur. Nous devons sÃ©curiser les corridors humanitaires et protÃ©ger les civils, particuliÃ¨rement les femmes et les enfants, souvent premiÃ¨res victimes de violences. Enfin, tout doit Ãªtre mis en Åuvre pour obtenir une paix rÃ©elle et durable. Madame la Commissaire, agissons maintenant et avec courage et compassion pour que le Soudan du Sud ne soit pas englouti dans un nouvel enfer de violence et de famine.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Leire PajÃ­n (S&D). â SeÃ±ora presidenta, seÃ±ora comisaria, el informe de las Naciones Unidas sobre ayuda humanitaria refleja que hay dos tendencias que estÃ¡n incrementando las necesidades humanitarias en el mundo: los conflictos y las devastadoras consecuencias del cambio climÃ¡tico.

Ambas se dan de forma desesperada en SudÃ¡n del Sur. Un paÃ­s expuesto a las consecuencias del cambio climÃ¡tico, a los desastres naturales y a la violencia, pero, con todo, lo mÃ¡s difÃ­cil de todo a lo que se enfrenta SudÃ¡n del Sur es a nuestra indiferencia. La indiferencia de quienes pretenden que reduzcamos nuestro compromiso con la ayuda humanitaria y con la cooperaciÃ³n al desarrollo. La indiferencia de quienes miran hacia otro lado o quienes votan en contra del informe sobre la financiaciÃ³n para el desarrollo sostenible.

SeÃ±orÃ­as, si seguimos reduciendo nuestro compromiso con la ayuda humanitaria, si seguimos reduciendo nuestro compromiso con la financiaciÃ³n para el desarrollo, si seguimos dando seÃ±ales de que los problemas del Sur Global no nos importan, no solo condenaremos a millones de personas, sino que, ademÃ¡s, acabaremos poniendo nuestra seguridad y nuestras vidas en peligro.

Â 
Â Â 
Â Â 

Procedura "catch-the-eye"

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Alessandra Moretti (S&D). â Signora Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, quasi dieci milioni di persone hanno bisogno di assistenza umanitaria in Sud Sudan, dove dilaga il colera, la fame e dove si registra il piÃ¹ alto tasso di mortalitÃ  neonatale al mondo. Un paese in cui lo stupro Ã¨ un'arma di guerra consueta.

A differenza di altri, questo Ã¨ un conflitto totalmente dimenticato. Il Sud Sudan Ã¨ sul precipizio di una nuova guerra totale. Tensioni politiche sfociate in violenze, milizie, bombardamenti tra ribelli ed esercito, profilazione etnica, detenzioni arbitrarie, attacchi a civili e ospedali, come quello di Medici Senza Frontiere, fatto esplodere con un bambino di nove mesi al suo interno. La popolazione fugge verso paesi fragili, aggravando cosÃ¬ crisi giÃ  esistenti.

Dobbiamo chiedere con forza al governo il ritorno al trattato di pace del 2018, la liberazione dei prigionieri politici, la fine delle ostilitÃ  e l'accesso sicuro per gli aiuti umanitari. Non facciamo calare l'attenzione.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Nikos Papandreou (S&D). â Madam President, Commissioner, this is the fifth time we speak about Sudan, and I'd like to mention something that has not been discussed. And that is the role of the foreign actors in worsening the disaster in Sudan.

We have a member of NATO, and a country that wants to be part of the SAFE architecture, and it's called TÃ¼rkiye. There's robust proof that TÃ¼rkiye is selling drones and military equipment to both sides of the conflict.

If TÃ¼rkiye wants to join the new architecture in the military, they should respect the sanctions on weapons to the war in Sudan. If they want to sit at the table, they should respect EU sanctions.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Sebastian Tynkkynen (ECR). â Madam President, the further escalation of violence in South Sudan has plunged the country even deeper into a devastating humanitarian crisis. Civilians are experiencing the full force of intensified clashes, leading to mass displacement, food insecurity and even a cholera outbreak.

South Sudan is already scarred with past civil wars. We do not want this young country to face another one. We must urgently call for a ceasefire and a renewed commitment to peace negotiations.

The European Union has a critical role in fostering diplomatic solutions that protect vulnerable populations. Failure to act risks prolonging this tragedy and destabilising the entire region. Let's act as mediators, call for peace and end the suffering.

Â 
Â Â 
Â Â 

(Fine della procedura "catch the eye")

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Ekaterina Zaharieva, Member of the Commission. â Madam President, honourable Members, this debate has shown the European Parliament's continued concerns and solidarity with the people of South Sudan. Your voices echoed the frustration and urgency felt by millions trapped in a crisis they did not choose. The international community cannot allow South Sudan to become a forgotten crisis. We must continue to speak out and to act through our diplomacy, through our development and humanitarian assistance.

I want to give you some facts, because in the debate it was said that the European Union is not actually investing enough and not supporting enough the humanitarian catastrophe that the people are facing there. As I said in my introduction, we are one of the biggest humanitarian supporters and development supporters for the South Sudanese people. Since South Sudan was declared an independent country, the European Union has invested over EURÂ 1.3 billion in development support for South Sudan and also a lot of humanitarian support: in 2024 it was EURÂ 110.2 million in humanitarian support, and this year EURÂ 71 million so far. I know that the needs are higher, and we are really trying to do as much as possible, but I think what is important is really to betterÂ coordinate the actions with the Member States as well.

The EU will continue working with regional partners, which is very important, to press for the implementation of the peace agreement. We will also remain firm in calling for respect of international humanitarian law and humanitarian access, and in our defence of civil space and human rights, including through the renewal of sanctions against those responsible for violence and obstruction. Above all, we must ensure that the people of South Sudan, especially its youth, women and displaced communities, are not silenced or sidelined in shaping their country's future. It's really important that they participate in shaping and forming, hopefully, democratic institutions. Thank you once again for your commitment and for keeping South Sudan high on our collective agenda.

Â 
Â Â 
Â Â 

SÄDI VADA: ROBERTS ZÄªLE
PriekÅ¡sÄdÄtÄja vietnieks

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â President. â The debate is closed.

Â 

15. Debate on cases of breaches of human rights, democracy and the rule of law (debate)

15.1. Media freedom in Georgia, particularly the case of Mzia Amaglobeli
Video of the speeches
Â 

Â Â PriekÅ¡sÄdÄtÄjs. â NÄkamais darba kÄrtÄ«bas punkts ir debates par septiÅiem rezolÅ«ciju priekÅ¡likumiem par mediju brÄ«vÄ«bu GruzijÄ, jo Ä«paÅ¡i Mzia Amaglobeli lietu (2025/2752(RSP)).

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Rasa JukneviÄienÄ, author. â Mr President, dear colleagues, journalist Mzia Amaglobeli has become a symbol of all Georgians imprisoned by the Bidzina Ivanishvili regime. A week ago, representatives of brave Georgians, who have been fighting for a European Georgia for over 200 days, attended the DROI Subcommittee hearings in the European Parliament. But yesterday, the Russian oligarch Ivanishvili's regime launched a full-scale legal assault against them.

The situation is getting worse every day. My speech this time will not be about the regime of the Kremlin's friends in Tbilisi. We have said everything about this in the resolutions adopted so far. We need to talk about us, the EU.

I do not understand the silence from the European Council, the Member States and the High Representative office. Mzia and her friends deserve respect and help. Serious sanctions against the regime are needed. Everyone who gets their hands dirty is afraid of them. We need to put pressure on the regime in every way and not ask them to return to the EU path anymore.

They will not return, because this regime is the Kremlin's dream. They lied to their people about their aspirations to the EU, they lied to us in the European Parliament and EU. Only new fair elections can help Georgians get their country back. EU political will is needed. There is almost no independent media left in Georgia, only Sputnik-type propaganda TV channels.

By the way, I do not understand why they, who spread poison about the EU, are still active here, in the European Parliament.

Let's act. The people of Georgia cannot be left alone.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Tobias Cremer, author. â MrÂ President, dear colleagues, media freedom is not a luxury, it is a democratic necessity. It shines light where corruption and autocracy hide. And that is precisely why the Georgian authorities have imprisoned one of the country's most respected journalists, Mzia Amaglobeli, for over 150 days now.

And Mzia's is not an isolated case. Over 400 Georgians â journalists, opposition figures, but also ordinary citizens â have been arrested, tortured or jailed.

Colleagues, the ruling Georgian Dream party fears the free press like a vampire fears the light, because they know that they can only survive in the shadows. In the shadow of the oligarch BidzinaÂ Ivanishvili, who does not dare to step into the open because he knows the Georgians don't want him, and in the shadow of Putin's regime, which uses disinformation, intimidation and manipulation, because it knows that Georgians would never choose to live like Russians have to if they have the free vote.

Colleagues, the light of a free press and a free society is the strongest weapon against tyranny, so we can only appeal to the Georgian authorities to step out of the shadows, drop the politically motivated charges and let Georgian people choose their future freely â in the light.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â MaÅgorzata Gosiewska, autorka. â Panie PrzewodniczÄcy! W Gruzji gasnÄ ÅwiatÅa demokracji. WÅadze zdominowane przez GruziÅskie Marzenie brutalnie tÅumiÄ prodemokratyczne protesty i prowadzÄ otwartÄ wojnÄ z wolnymi mediami.

Mzia Amaglobeli â szanowana dziennikarka â zostaÅa aresztowana tylko za to, Å¼e odwaÅ¼yÅa siÄ zaprotestowaÄ. W swoim dramatycznym apelu powiedziaÅa âNie zaakceptujÄ agendy reÅ¼imu. ProwadzÄ strajk gÅodowy. WolnoÅÄ jest cenniejsza niÅ¼ Å¼ycieâ.

WolnoÅÄ jest cenniejsza niÅ¼ Å¼ycie.

JuÅ¼ prawie 4 lata wiÄÅºniem politycznym reÅ¼imu Iwaniszwilego jest byÅy prezydent Gruzji â Saakaszwili. GruziÅskie Marzenie, kierowane zza kulis przez BidzinÄ Iwaniszwilego, odwrÃ³ciÅo GruzjÄ od Europy.

Ten kraj â aspirujÄcy niegdyÅ do Unii Europejskiej i NATO â staÅ siÄ satelitÄ Moskwy.

Nie moÅ¼emy biernie siÄ temu przyglÄdaÄ. Gruzini walczÄ o podstawowe wartoÅci. WalczÄ o demokracjÄ, o wolnoÅÄ sÅowa, o normalne Å¼ycie. Popieranie dyktatury, ulegÅoÅÄ wobec niej to zbrodnia. ObojÄtnoÅÄ i milczenie to rÃ³wnieÅ¼ zbrodnia, szanowni paÅstwo.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Dainius Å½alimas, author. â MrÂ President, dear colleagues in Georgia, we are witnessing the attacks on the rule of law, independent media, NGOs, freedoms of assembly and association. The Georgian nightmare is rapidly transforming the country into autocracy. A single-party system, apparently, is not compatible with pluralistic democracy.

Several times, our Parliament has already demanded the immediate release of all political prisoners and called for new, free and fair parliamentary elections. This is the only way to restore democracy. Our Parliament has also declared the legitimacy of the current parliament and president in Georgia. However, not many Member States follow the Parliament's line, nor the Commission, which supports, by the way, the accreditation of ambassadors to the illegitimate President of Georgia. Only a few Member States have imposed sanctions.

Therefore, the EU seems to be treating Georgia as a lost country. But this is a big mistake. The Georgian civil society continues to fight. They have not lost themselves, nor their European aspirations. We cannot abandon them if we really seek to support democracy.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Lena Schilling, author. â MrÂ President, dear colleagues, Mzia Amaghlobeli is Georgia's first and only journalist imprisoned as a political detainee. Her arrest is clearly political and based on fabricated charges. I was in Georgia and I attended one of her trials. And I can tell you something: her case is a direct attack on press freedom. Mzia symbolises a movement that, despite repression and intimidation, continues to fight for democracy and a European future. She is targeted because she stands for truth and refuses to be silenced. Today, hundreds of political prisoners, opposition leaders, activists and artists are jailed for opposing a system dismantling democracy and enforcing Russian control.

The people of Georgia are fighting for the very values this Parliament stands for: freedom, human rights and the rule of law. Supporting Mzia means standing for a free, democratic and European Georgia, and we need to step up here to support her and all the people in Georgia.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Danilo Della Valle, autore. â Signor Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, siamo qui a votare l'ennesima risoluzione che parla di Georgia.

Voglio premettere che la libertÃ  di stampa Ã¨ importante, Ã¨ un valore imprescindibile e che Ã¨ giusto chiedere la liberazione di Amaglobeli. PerÃ² trovo insopportabile utilizzare i diritti umani e la retorica democratica per meri scopi geopolitici e attaccare i paesi che hanno scelto una politica estera diversa da quella che piace a Washington, von der Leyen, Merz o Macron.

Mi piacerebbe parlare di tutti i giornalisti che si trovano in carcere per il loro lavoro. A Gaza piÃ¹ di 200 sono stati uccisi dal 7 ottobre ad oggi e 34 si trovano nelle carceri israeliane. Penso a Shireen Abu Akleh, giornalista americano-palestinese uccisa deliberatamente dall'esercito mentre svolgeva il suo lavoro a Jenin. Questi nomi perÃ² non hanno la dignitÃ  di essere considerati qui dentro.

Esprimete il vostro sdegno per la Georgia, mentre siete silenti con Israele che attacca le tv iraniane in diretta mondiale. Qualcuno di voi osa dire anche che sta facendo il lavoro sporco per noi. Ma quale sarebbe il lavoro sporco da fare? Uccidere migliaia di donne e bambine innocenti, farli morire di fame?

Vi invito a parlare al singolare, perchÃ© milioni di persone in Europa stanno protestando in tutti i modi contro il genocidio in corso a Gaza e noi non saremo complici del vostro sporco intento genocidio.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Petr Bystron, Verfasser. â Herr PrÃ¤sident, liebe Kollegen! Das EUâParlament setzt sich fÃ¼r Redefreiheit ein und fÃ¼r eine NGOâAktivistin, die einen Polizisten angegriffen hat und deswegen im GefÃ¤ngnis sitzt. Wenn Ihnen wirklich die Redefreiheit und unabhÃ¤ngige Journalisten am Herzen liegen, die im GefÃ¤ngnis sitzen, dann schauen Sie erst mal in die eigenen Reihen, in die EU. Wir haben einen David Bendels, einen Chefredakteur in Deutschland, der sieben Monate Haft auf BewÃ¤hrung bekommen hat, nur weil er eine Collage verÃ¶ffentlicht hat, auf der die damalige Innenministerin Nancy Faeser draufstand mit dem Spruch: âIch hasse Meinungsfreiheitâ. Das ist der Zustand der Meinungsfreiheit hier in der EU, in Deutschland.

Oder Shlomo FinkelsteinÂ â zwÃ¶lf Monate Verurteilung, neun Monate im Knast. Ein YouTuber, der hat keine Polizisten verprÃ¼gelt, der hat sich nur fÃ¼r die Meinungsfreiheit ausgesprochen. Oder kehren wir auch vor der TÃ¼r der EU: Tommy Robinson, ein echter Journalist, der sich fÃ¼r die Meinungsfreiheit einsetzt; auch der saÃ mehrere Monate im GefÃ¤ngnis, nur damit er seinen Film âSilencedâ nicht gezeigt hat.

Also bitte, setzen Sie sich wirklich fÃ¼r Journalisten ein! UnterstÃ¼tzen Sie keine NGOs im Ausland, die nur einem regime change dienen sollen!

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Liudas MaÅ¾ylis, PPE frakcijos vardu. â Gerbiamas Pirmininke, komisare, kolegos. Mzijos Amaglobeli atvejis signalizuoja apie dabartinÄs Sakartvelo valdÅ¾ios antidemokratinÄ¯ veiksenos Å¡ablonÄ, kai su asmenimis, Å¾iniasklaida ir pilietine visuomene susidorojama per teisminÄ valdÅ¾iÄ. O tai nusiÅ¾iÅ«rÄta nuo kaimyniniÅ³ Baltarusijos ar Rusijos, o greiÄiausiai â ir Rusijos tiesiogiai inspiruota. Taip selektyvus teisinis spaudimas tampa reÅ¾imo modusÂ operandi. MinÄtos Å¾urnalistÄs sulaikymas bei paskirta neadekvati laisvÄs atÄmimo bausmÄ jau nÄra pavienis atvejis â tai autoritarinÄs sistemos bruoÅ¾as. TodÄl, kol dar Sakartvelo neiÅ¡tiko visiÅ¡kas institucinis Å¾lugimas, privalome didinti spaudimÄ konkretiems autoritarinio reÅ¾imo Ä¯gyvendintojams, susitariant dÄl tiksliniÅ³ sankcijÅ³. Sakartvelo Å¾monÄs, eidami Ä¯ gatves, rizikuodami savo gyvybÄmis Ä¯rodo, kad jÅ³ provakarietiÅ¡ka orientacija nÄra pasikeitusi. TodÄl mes turime daryti viskÄ, kad padÄtume jÄ iÅ¡saugoti.

Â 
Â Â
